skill.name: sci-paper-reviewer
skill.description: Simulates a strict SCI peer-review workflow; trigger when a user uploads or pastes a manuscript (PDF/DOC/DOCX/TXT) and requests an innovation score (1-12) plus experimental-logic vulnerability checks and revision suggestions.
skill.summary: Academic manuscript peer-review simulator that parses PDF/Word/TXT files and produces structured innovation scoring and experimental logic audits.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 46 static analysis findings are false positives. The 25 'external_commands' findings appear in Markdown code blocks showing example pip install and script usage (non-executable documentation). The 11 'weak cryptographic algorithm' and 'system reconnaissance' findings are strings in audit JSON and plain text descriptions, not actual code. The 6 filesystem findings are legitimate document parser output operations (writing extracted text to files). The 3 network findings are XML namespace URIs for DOCX parsing and a GitHub source attribution link. No evidence of malicious intent, data exfiltration, or prompt injection was found.

low_findings.title: File write operations in document parser
low_findings.description: The parser writes extracted content to output files and JSON summary files. These are legitimate operations for a document parser - writing parsed text output is expected functionality. Output file paths are derived from input file paths using Path.with_suffix(), not from user-supplied path traversal sequences.

content.user_title: Review Academic Papers with SCI-Grade Analysis
content.value_statement: Researchers often struggle to get structured, rigorous feedback before submitting manuscripts to journals. This skill parses PDF, Word, and TXT academic papers and applies a strict peer-review framework with innovation scoring, experimental logic vulnerability checks, and numbered revision suggestions.
actual_capabilities: Parses PDF documents using multiple extraction libraries (pdfplumber, pypdf, PyMuPDF, PyPDF2) with automatic fallback
Parses DOCX files including table content extraction, with basic DOC fallback support
Extracts academic paper sections (Abstract, Introduction, Methods, Results, Discussion, Conclusion) from unstructured text
Applies a 1-12 innovation scoring rubric based on originality, theory extension, and translational path
Screens Results sections for logic vulnerabilities: false positive risk, mechanism breaks, and control failures
Generates numbered, concrete experimental modification suggestions tied to specific claims
limitations: Cannot process scanned PDFs or image-based documents (no OCR capability)
Legacy .doc format support is limited and may produce degraded output
Innovation scoring reflects the skill's rubric, not official journal reviewer standards
Cannot access subscription-based journals or paywalled content

use_cases.title: Pre-submission manuscript polish
use_cases.description: A graduate student uploads their manuscript PDF and requests a structured review before journal submission. The skill parses the document, assigns an innovation score, and provides numbered revision suggestions.

use_cases.title: Experimental design audit for ongoing research
use_cases.description: A principal investigator pastes a draft Results section and asks the skill to screen for false positive risks, missing controls, and mechanism breaks before committing to expensive validation experiments.

use_cases.title: Journal club paper evaluation
use_cases.description: A journal club member uploads a recently published paper and requests a structured breakdown including research type classification, innovation assessment, and methodology critique for group discussion.
use_cases.target_user: Journal club participants and academic discussion groups

prompt_templates.title: Quick manuscript review
prompt_templates.prompt: Please review this manuscript as a strict SCI reviewer. Classify the research type, evaluate innovation using the 1-12 rubric, screen Results for logic vulnerabilities, and provide numbered modification suggestions.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has manuscript text ready to paste

prompt_templates.title: File-based review with document parser
prompt_templates.prompt: Parse the attached manuscript file using the enhanced document parser and perform a full SCI-style review. Include the document information, innovation evaluation, and experimental modification suggestions in the output.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a PDF or DOCX file to upload

prompt_templates.title: Results-focused methodology audit
prompt_templates.prompt: I need a focused methodology audit of my Results section. Check for: (1) false positive risks from single-assay claims, (2) mechanism breaks where correlation is presented as causation, and (3) missing double-negative controls. Provide only experimental modification suggestions numbered as 2.1, 2.2, etc.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants targeted scrutiny of experimental methods

prompt_templates.title: Comparative innovation scoring with rubric
prompt_templates.prompt: I have two related manuscripts on the same topic. Score each using the 1-12 innovation rubric and compare them side by side. For each, provide: research type classification, innovation score with rationale per rubric category, and the top 3 experimental modifications needed.

output_examples.input: Please review this manuscript abstract for innovation score and logic vulnerabilities.
output_examples.output: The skill processes the abstract text and returns a structured review with: research type classification (e.g., Basic Medical Research), an innovation score with rationale mapped to the rubric dimensions, and flagged logic vulnerabilities such as missing orthogonal validation for key claims.

output_examples.input: Parse this PDF and check the Results section for control failures.
output_examples.output: The skill parses the PDF using the best available extraction library, identifies the Results section via section heading matching, and produces a vulnerability report. Example finding: '2.1 - Control Failure: Figure 3 knockdown experiment lacks a double-negative control (scrambled siRNA + vehicle). Add this control to rule off-target effects.'

best_practices: Provide the full manuscript text when possible, as section extraction is more accurate with complete content
Specify which section to focus on (Results, Methods, or full review) to get the most relevant output
Use the file-based workflow (parser + review) for complex PDFs with tables and multi-column layouts
anti_patterns: Do not paste only the abstract and expect a thorough methodology audit - the skill needs the Results section to screen for experimental logic vulnerabilities
Do not request reviews of papers outside your expertise area - the skill labels research types but does not verify domain-specific factual accuracy
Do not rely on the innovation score as an official journal ranking - it is a rubric-based approximation, not a real peer review

faq.question: What file formats does the parser support?
faq.answer: The parser supports PDF, DOCX, DOC, and TXT files. PDFs are processed using multiple extraction libraries with automatic fallback. DOCX files support full text and table extraction. Legacy DOC format has limited support.

faq.question: Can the skill review paywalled or subscription-based papers?
faq.answer: No. The skill only processes files you provide or text you paste. It cannot access subscription journals, paywalled content, or any external databases.

faq.question: Does this skill replace actual peer review from a journal?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides a structured rubric-based analysis as a preparatory tool. It cannot replace the domain expertise, editorial judgment, or ethical oversight of real peer review.

faq.question: How is the innovation score of 1-12 calculated?
faq.answer: The score uses a rubric with four tiers: Major Original (9-12), Theory Extension (5-8), Potential Application Value (4-7), and Validation Study (1-4). The skill evaluates originality, mechanism novelty, and translational path from the manuscript content.

faq.question: Can the skill handle non-English manuscripts?
faq.answer: The parser can extract text from any language, but the skill analysis works best with English-language academic writing. Section extraction patterns are tuned for English section headings.

faq.question: What happens if my paper has no Results section?
faq.answer: The skill will note that the Results section was not found and will skip logic vulnerability screening. The review will focus on other available sections (Abstract, Introduction, Discussion) and provide an innovation score based on available content.
